sections_cited: Sections 194-A and 194-B of the Customs Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 3664 of 2020 ___________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge(s) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For hearing of CMA No.15317/2020. 2. For hearing of main case. 13.02.2025 Ms. Dil Khurram Shaheen, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Zuhaib Ahmed, advocate holding brief for Mr. Khalid Rajpar, advocate for the respondent. Ms. Alizeh Bashir, Assistant Attorney General. This is a petition seeking implementation of an order passed by the Customs Appellate Tribunal at Karachi. It appears that vide order dated 09.08.2024 passed by the honourable Supreme Court whereby it has been held the Customs Appellate Tribunal has the power to execute orders passed by it while exercising jurisdiction under Sections 194-A and 194-B of the Customs Act, 1969, and consequently, since an adequate remedy is provided by law, the writ jurisdiction of the High Court cannot be invoked for executing orders passed by the Tribunal. In view of such position, this petition is dismissed as not maintainable with pending applications, whereas the petitioner is at liberty to seek appropriate remedy as observed in the above order of the honourable Supreme Court. Judge Judge Khuhro/PA
